name: Planets and the Solar System
description: The 8 planets, dwarf planets, the Sun, and other features of our solar system

questions.question: How many planets are in our solar system?
questions.explanation: There are 8 officially recognized planets since 2006, when Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet. Before that, schoolchildren learned 9.

questions.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury orbits just 36 million miles from the Sun. Despite being closest, it is not the hottest planet -- Venus is, thanks to its runaway greenhouse effect.

questions.question: Which planet is second from the Sun and the hottest in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Venus reaches surface temperatures of about 900 degrees F (475 degrees C) -- hot enough to melt lead. It also rotates backward compared to most planets, so the Sun rises in the west.

questions.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is so massive that over 1,300 Earths could fit inside it. Its Great Red Spot is a storm that has been raging for at least 350 years and is wider than Earth.

questions.question: Which planet is famous for its prominent ring system?
questions.explanation: Saturn's rings are made mostly of ice and rock particles, ranging from tiny grains to house-sized chunks. Despite their enormous width, the rings are only about 30 feet thick in most places.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars gets its red color from iron oxide (rust) in its soil. It has the tallest volcano in the solar system -- Olympus Mons, standing about 72,000 feet high, nearly 2.5 times the height of Everest.

questions.question: Which planet rotates on its side with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees?
questions.explanation: Uranus essentially rolls around the Sun on its side, likely due to a massive collision early in its history. This means its poles take turns pointing directly at the Sun, giving it extreme 42-year-long seasons.

questions.question: What is the farthest planet from the Sun in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Neptune orbits about 2.8 billion miles from the Sun. It has the fastest winds of any planet, reaching over 1,200 mph. One Neptune year lasts about 165 Earth years.

questions.question: What planet was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006?
questions.explanation: The International Astronomical Union reclassified Pluto because it has not 'cleared the neighborhood' around its orbit. Pluto is smaller than Earth's Moon and has a heart-shaped nitrogen ice plain called Tombaugh Regio.

questions.question: What is the largest object in the asteroid belt and was the first dwarf planet to be visited by a spacecraft?
questions.explanation: Ceres makes up about a third of the asteroid belt's total mass. NASA's Dawn spacecraft orbited it in 2015 and discovered bright spots that turned out to be salt deposits from an underground brine reservoir.

questions.question: What dwarf planet beyond Pluto's orbit is roughly the same size as Pluto and helped trigger Pluto's reclassification?
questions.explanation: Eris was discovered in 2005 and is about 27% more massive than Pluto. Its discovery forced astronomers to either call it a 10th planet or redefine what 'planet' means -- they chose the latter.

questions.question: The asteroid belt lies between the orbits of which two planets?
questions.explanation: Despite what movies show, the asteroid belt is mostly empty space. If you combined all the asteroids together, their total mass would be less than 4% of our Moon's mass.
